title: Ne or No Ne
subtitle: In the past tense, does the subject take 'ne'?
question: Does this past-tense sentence take 'ne'?
tip: Read the verb, not the subject. Ask 'did this action land on an object?' Yes means ne, no means no ne. Then watch for the three liars: bola, laaya, bhoola look transitive but never take ne.

left.label: NE
bullets: Transitive perfective past takes ne: maine khaaya, usne padha, Ram ne chai pi.
If the action lands on an object (ate what, read what), it is transitive: khaana, peena, padhna, likhna, dekhna, sunna.
Compound verbs inherit the main verb's transitivity: maine kar liya, usne de diya.
The verb then agrees with the OBJECT's gender, not the subject: maine roti khaayi, maine paani piya.

right.label: NO NE
bullets: Intransitive verbs never take ne: woh gaya, main soya, bachcha roya, woh baitha.
If there is no object (go, come, sleep, sit, cry, fall, run), the verb is intransitive.
Three transitive-looking liars also take NO ne: bolna (woh bola), laana (main laaya), bhoolna (main bhool gaya).
Here the verb agrees with the SUBJECT as normal: woh aayi, main soyi.

items.explain: khaana (to eat) is transitive and past, so the subject takes ne.
jaana (to go) is intransitive, so no ne and the verb agrees with the subject.
padhna (to read) is transitive and past, so the subject gets ne (us / usne).
sona (to sleep) is intransitive, so no ne.
peena (to drink) is transitive, so even a named subject takes ne.
rona (to cry) is intransitive, so no ne.
likhna (to write) is transitive, so the subject takes ne.
baithna (to sit) is intransitive, so no ne.
dekhna (to watch) is transitive, so the subject gets ne.
bolna (to speak) looks transitive but is one of the exceptions that takes NO ne.
sunna (to hear) is transitive, so the subject takes ne (ham / humne).

items.hint: I got up in the morning
items.explain: uthna (to get up) is intransitive, so no ne.
karna (to do) is transitive, so the subject takes ne.
girna (to fall) is intransitive, so no ne.
banaana (to make) is transitive, so the subject gets ne.
laana (to bring) looks transitive but is an exception that takes NO ne.
kholna (to open) is transitive, so the subject takes ne.
daudna (to run) is intransitive, so no ne.
bhoolna (to forget) looks transitive but is an exception that takes NO ne.
poochna (to ask) is transitive, so the subject gets ne.
pahunchna (to arrive) is intransitive, so no ne.
chalaana (to drive) is transitive, so the subject takes ne.
hansna (to laugh) is intransitive, so no ne.
bolna (to speak) is one of the exceptions that takes NO ne, so the subject stays tum (not tumne) and the verb agrees with it.
khareedna (to buy) is transitive, so the subject gets ne.
lautna (to return) is intransitive, so no ne.
khaana is transitive, so ne; the verb ends in -i to agree with roti (feminine).

items.hint: I lay down on the bed
items.explain: letna (to lie down) is intransitive, so no ne.
gaana (to sing) is transitive, so the subject takes ne.
baithna (to sit) is intransitive, so no ne.
laana (to bring) is an exception that takes NO ne, so the subject stays main (not maine) and the verb agrees with it.
jeetna (to win) is transitive here, so the subject takes ne (ham / humne).
aana (to come) is intransitive, so no ne.
bhejna (to send) is transitive, so the subject gets ne.
pahunchna (to arrive) is intransitive, so no ne.
peena is transitive, so ne; the verb ends in -a to agree with paani (masculine).
rukna (to stop) is intransitive, so no ne.
kaatna (to cut) is transitive, so the subject gets ne.
jaagna (to stay awake) is intransitive, so no ne.
bhoolna (to forget) is an exception that takes NO ne, so the subject stays tum (not tumne) and the verb agrees with it.
dhona (to wash) is transitive, so the subject takes ne.
naachna (to dance) is intransitive, so no ne.
karna (band karna, to close) is transitive, so the subject gets ne.
ghabraana (to get nervous) is intransitive, so no ne.
seekhna (to learn) is transitive, so the subject takes ne (ham / humne).
milna (to meet) is intransitive in Hindi, so no ne.
banaana is transitive, so ne; the verb ends in -i to agree with chai (feminine).
chalna (to walk) is intransitive, so no ne.
uthaana (to lift) is transitive, so the subject gets ne.
jaagna (to wake up) is intransitive, so no ne.
laana (to bring) is an exception that takes NO ne, so the subject stays tum (not tumne) and the verb agrees with it.

items.hint: I got all the work done
items.explain: the compound kar liya inherits transitivity from karna, so the subject takes ne.
